best_edges.evidence: pullback_long is the largest real edge: 31 trades, $2415.3 PnL, 252.47 PnL/exposure hour, median hold 12.0m, no slow 12h trades. Tail is material but not dominant: worst MAE -403.2 and avg MAE -110.16.

best_edges.action: protect; test TP/exposure expansion only after tail guard validation
best_edges.evidence: charm_wall_breakaway_long has only 5 trades but strong payoff quality: $985.6 PnL, 234.36 PnL/exposure hour, median MFE 146.2, median TP 12.0, no slow 12h trades. Sample is small and avg MAE is high at -148.96.

best_edges.action: protect as a fallback edge, but avoid broad risk pruning
best_edges.evidence: breakout_long_1dte produced 20 trades and $1305.0 PnL with 168.76 PnL/exposure hour and no slow 12h trades. It is a major contributor when VALID_1DTE_ONLY is present, but avg MAE -123.86 and worst MAE -375.7 require monitoring.

best_edges.action: protect cleaner efficient subset; expand with caution
best_edges.evidence: squeeze_long generated 10 trades, $721.0 PnL, 187.16 PnL/exposure hour, worst MAE only -208.25 versus larger profiles. Median setup quality 69.16 and option-map constraints suggest a cleaner momentum subset.

call_wall_acceptance_long has only 1 trade but it was efficient: $91 PnL, 455 PnL/exposure hour, worst MAE -11.7, median ADX 42.75 and setup quality 72.5. This is too small to trust but good enough to run controlled expansion.

broken_edges.action: do not expand until tail-specific gating is tested
broken_edges.evidence: 34 trades and $1237.7 PnL are useful, but it owns the worst MAE at -527.0. The worst row had ES-VWAP distance 26.2, negative RSI slope, ADX below 18, 80.3m hold, and no favorable path before MAE.

broken_edges.action: tighten or isolate stale fallback before profit expansion
broken_edges.evidence: STALE_0DTE contributed only $162.8 across 5 trades. breakout_long_no_0dte stale subset had avg MAE -210.6, worst MAE -250.25, median hold 52.6m, and only 40.5 PnL/exposure hour.

broken_edges.action: keep enabled only as a small defensive fallback unless efficiency improves
broken_edges.evidence: pullback_long_no_0dte stale subset produced 3 trades, $91.8 PnL, 83.2 PnL/exposure hour, and median hold 16.35m. It is positive but weak versus the main VALID_0DTE pullback edge.

broken_edges.action: freeze unless explicitly testing dormant/router activation
broken_edges.evidence: breakout_long, late_day_pin_fade, negative_gamma_momentum_long, trend_day_pullback_long, wall_breakout_retest_long, and wall_reclaim_long are router-listed but produced zero trades in this run; optimizing thresholds without observed fills is likely noise.

market_regime_read: The current leader is a high-win, long-only SPX continuation/pullback system over a short 12.3 calendar-day window. Most profit comes from VALID_0DTE and VALID_1DTE_ONLY states, with fast winners contributing 74.9% of PnL and 65 trades under 15 minutes. The dominant regime appears to be controlled upside continuation above VWAP, not mean reversion. Risk is hidden in no-stop-loss underwater paths: median TP touch is fast at 3.9m, but only 0.9% touch TP before MAE and the worst MAE rows still exit green after long underwater holds. Profit expansion should therefore prioritize increasing PnL per exposure hour and convex TP capture in already-profitable profiles, while avoiding changes that add slow-to-profit exposure or widen the charm_pin tail.

new_strategy_ideas.edge: Several worst MAE rows eventually recover after deep underwater movement; a separate intratrade rescue/tighten policy could reduce capital lockup without blocking entries.
new_strategy_ideas.failure_mode: May cut trades that currently recover to profit, reducing the artificially high win rate and changing strategy economics.
new_strategy_ideas.name: MAE-Aware Recovery Exit Overlay
new_strategy_ideas.required_data: Intratrade path MAE/MFE timing, first 15m MFE/MAE, time since entry, current option price versus effective TP.
new_strategy_ideas.trigger: Position fails to make early positive progress and reaches a path profile similar to the worst MAE cluster: no positive-before-MAE, weak first 15m MFE, and long hold risk.
new_strategy_ideas.validation_metric: Worst MAE and exposure hours fall materially while net profit remains at least 95-98% of baseline and PnL/exposure hour improves.

new_strategy_ideas.edge: call_wall_acceptance_long showed a very clean single trade with high ADX and low MAE; a dedicated acceptance profile could harvest high-confidence call-wall continuation without broadening noisy pullbacks.
new_strategy_ideas.failure_mode: Only one observed trade, so activation may overfit or cannibalize better profiles.
new_strategy_ideas.name: High-ADX Call Wall Acceptance Expansion
new_strategy_ideas.required_data: Call wall distance/acceptance, option call-volume increase, ADX, ES-VWAP distance/slope, setup quality.
new_strategy_ideas.trigger: VALID_0DTE with accepted upside call wall, strong ADX, and constructive ES-VWAP structure.
new_strategy_ideas.validation_metric: At least 5-10 additional trades with PnL/exposure hour above 250, avg MAE below baseline, and no degradation in pullback_long fills.

new_strategy_ideas.edge: Dormant pullback_long_1dte may capture 1DTE pullbacks currently routed only to breakout_long_1dte, potentially increasing VALID_1DTE_ONLY breadth.
new_strategy_ideas.failure_mode: Could duplicate breakout_long_1dte and add lower-quality 1DTE exposure with high MAE.
new_strategy_ideas.name: 1DTE Pullback Companion Test
new_strategy_ideas.required_data: VALID_1DTE_ONLY state, pullback/reclaim structure, 1DTE option map, ES-VWAP distance, RSI/ADX momentum.
new_strategy_ideas.trigger: 1DTE-only data state with pullback/reclaim conditions rather than breakout-only conditions.
new_strategy_ideas.validation_metric: VALID_1DTE_ONLY PnL rises without worsening worst MAE beyond -400 or reducing PnL/exposure hour below breakout_long_1dte baseline.

new_strategy_ideas.edge: Dormant delta_charm_breakout_long could isolate high-convexity dealer-flow breakouts when call/DEX/charm data aligns.
new_strategy_ideas.failure_mode: May overlap squeeze_long or breakout_long_1dte and increase chase entries near exhaustion.
new_strategy_ideas.name: Delta Charm Breakout Activation Probe
new_strategy_ideas.required_data: Net charm, forward DEX ratio, call-put volume imbalance, gamma regime score, ES-VWAP slope/acceleration, rolling-high distance.
new_strategy_ideas.trigger: VALID_0DTE with dealer-flow confirmation and breakout structure above VWAP.
new_strategy_ideas.validation_metric: Incremental trades show PnL/exposure hour above 225, median hold below 15m, and worst MAE below current system worst.

next_experiments.config_bias: Loosen profit capture only inside the strongest existing edges, not globally. Bias toward pullback_long and charm_wall_breakaway_long TP/quality bands where MFE materially exceeds configured TP, while preserving current entry structure.
next_experiments.goal: Increase total PnL and PnL/exposure hour without increasing tail MAE.
next_experiments.hypothesis: pullback_long and charm_wall_breakaway_long have enough MFE to support slightly larger realized exits or better dynamic TP selection; expanding winners is safer than adding new router breadth first.
next_experiments.name: Winner TP Harvest Expansion
next_experiments.primary_metric: risk-adjusted score with PnL/exposure hour above 210 and worst MAE not worse than -527
next_experiments.risk: Longer holds may reduce PnL/exposure hour and expose no-stop positions to deeper underwater paths.

next_experiments.config_bias: Run a narrow expansion around call_wall_acceptance_long entry permissiveness while keeping high-quality ADX/VWAP/acceptance constraints. Avoid enabling unrelated dormant profiles in the same run.
next_experiments.goal: Determine whether the clean call-wall edge can scale beyond one trade.
next_experiments.hypothesis: The call wall acceptance profile is under-sampled, but the observed trade had very low MAE and high efficiency; modestly increasing its fill count may improve profit without adding large tail exposure.
next_experiments.name: Call Wall Acceptance Scale Test
next_experiments.primary_metric: additional call_wall_acceptance_long trades with PnL/exposure hour above 250 and avg MAE below -75
next_experiments.risk: One-trade evidence is weak; looser acceptance could admit late breakout chases.

next_experiments.config_bias: Test 1DTE breadth by adding a controlled pullback_long_1dte router activation as a separate experiment, not mixed with VALID_0DTE changes. Keep breakout_long_1dte protected as the baseline comparator.
next_experiments.goal: Increase VALID_1DTE_ONLY opportunity count while maintaining current 1DTE efficiency.
next_experiments.hypothesis: VALID_1DTE_ONLY contributes $1305 across 20 trades using only breakout_long_1dte; a pullback companion may harvest missed non-breakout continuation without harming 0DTE edges.
next_experiments.name: 1DTE Pullback Router Probe
next_experiments.primary_metric: VALID_1DTE_ONLY net PnL and PnL/exposure hour improve, with worst MAE no worse than -400
next_experiments.risk: The new 1DTE profile may duplicate or front-run breakout entries and increase exposure without incremental edge.

next_experiments.config_bias: Use surgical charm_pin tail controls informed by extended-VWAP rejection near-misses, but avoid broad setup-quality floors that would remove too much PnL. Prefer thresholds that catch ES-VWAP overextension and failed early progress signatures.
next_experiments.goal: Reduce worst MAE from charm_pin_pullback_long while preserving at least 90% of its PnL.
next_experiments.hypothesis: The worst charm_pin tail is an overextended VWAP rejection with weak RSI/ADX context; surgical rejection criteria can remove the deepest MAE rows without killing the whole charm-pin edge.
next_experiments.name: Charm Pin Tail Isolation
next_experiments.primary_metric: charm_pin_pullback_long worst MAE improves by at least 20% while total net profit remains at least 98% of baseline
next_experiments.risk: Good extended-VWAP winners exist, so blunt extension filters will likely over-prune fast profitable trades.

next_experiments.config_bias: Treat STALE_0DTE and EXPIRED_0DTE as separate from VALID_0DTE. Test stricter fallback routing/quality only for no-0DTE profiles, leaving VALID_0DTE profiles unchanged.
next_experiments.goal: Improve fallback efficiency and reduce stale-state exposure drag.
next_experiments.hypothesis: No-0DTE fallback trades are positive but inefficient, especially stale breakout fallback; stricter fallback routing can raise PnL/exposure hour without materially hurting total PnL.
next_experiments.name: No-0DTE Fallback Efficiency Gate
next_experiments.primary_metric: fallback PnL/exposure hour above 150 with avg MAE below baseline fallback MAE and total net profit at least 98% of baseline
next_experiments.risk: Sample is only 6 fallback trades, so any improvement may be unstable.

research_notes: Do not optimize around the 100% win rate; in a no-stop-loss system it mainly means positions are allowed to wait. The better objective is PnL per exposure hour with bounded MAE. The largest protected edge is pullback_long, followed by 1DTE breakout and charm_wall_breakaway. The most dangerous active edge is charm_pin_pullback_long because it contributes useful PnL but owns the -527 MAE tail. Hidden signature candidates are not yet clean enough for broad pruning because top retained-PnL filters still leave worst MAE or sacrifice 18-28% of profit. Upside TP extension report found no formal candidates, so TP expansion should be profile-scoped and validated empirically rather than global.
risk_controls: Require any profit-expansion run to keep worst MAE no worse than current -527 and preferably improve average MAE from -101.7.
Reject experiments that improve net profit only by materially increasing exposure hours or median hold time.
Protect fast-winner fingerprints: avoid broad filters on VWAP extension, setup score, or entry-veto points because fast winners and extended-VWAP winners overlap those regions.
Keep VALID_0DTE and 1DTE experiments isolated from stale/no-0DTE fallback experiments so routing effects are attributable.
Treat one-trade edges such as call_wall_acceptance_long and EXPIRED_0DTE breakout fallback as probes, not proven production edges.
Do not spend config budget tuning zero-trade active profiles unless the experiment is explicitly a router/dormant activation test.
